title: 2022.01.07-1.77.2-nb  version  does not look good on 4k monitor
author: cloud-66
body_snippet:
<!--- Provide a general summary of the issue in the Title above -->
new 2022.01.07-1.77.2-nb mRemoteNG Portable  version  on 4k monitor does not scale well  
I am using 200% scale


## Current Behavior
two problems
1. menu elements not clean for 4k resolution, it looks like  enlarged image
2. terminal fonts too big, looks like scaled twice
![Screenshot_42](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/41143306/149364484-2c48b35e-97dc-49e6-8788-372efbe0b772.png)

## Old version
mRemoteNG....

iterations.description: The application's UI does not scale correctly on 4K monitors, leading to a poor user experience, despite existing DPI awareness implementations.
Bulk verified: commit a1717026 exists (MEDIUM confidence)

comments.author: simonai1254
comments.snippet:
Do you have Scaling enabled in windows or not? (Have a look at Windows Settings > System > Display > Select the corresponding display > Scale & Layout).
I did not notice this effect, but am running with 100% scaling. If it's not at 100% this means that everything is scaled, and I guess this affects the images as well. Maybe one would need higher resolution icons for the menu if this really is an issue...

comments.author: cloud-66
comments.snippet: I use windows 200% scaling. Your 100% mean that you don’t use scaling

comments.author: simonai1254
comments.snippet: In this case I guess one should use a little higher res images if that's supported by .net 6 desktop to get the icons a little more crisp on the screen

comments.author: cloud-66
comments.snippet: There is not only icons problem but fonts also. Just look on full size screenshot

comments.author: lpn-ch
comments.snippet:
I confirm the same issue on my 4K monitor scaled at 200%. Portable version 1.77.1 works perfect out of box, no custom DPI settings whatsoever.

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/25384886/153447604-d4bd8f06-67f3-448d-9c17-ed51f6e8dd7b.png)

Version 1.77.2-nb looks fuzzy.

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/25384886/153710737-d367989f-d3f4-4a8c-a7ca-7bea147ac474.png)

comments.author: garpunkal
comments.snippet:
I am seeing the same issue on 1.77.2 but not on 1.77.1.

I'm on 4k 200%, if I use on my other monitors (2k and 1k), they look fine.

comments.author: robertpopa22
comments.snippet:
**Fix available for testing**

**Commit:** `a1717026` (fork `robertpopa22/mRemoteNG`, branch `main`)
**What changed:** 2022.01.07-1.77.2-nb  version  does not look good on 4k moni

A beta build (v1.81.0-beta.4) including this fix is available from the fork's Releases page.

notes:
AI triage (gemini): The application's UI does not scale correctly on 4K monitors, leading to a poor user experience, despite existing DPI awareness implementations.
Approach: Verify and ensure consistent `PerMonitorV2` DPI awareness is correctly enabled at the application's entry point. Review and adjust existing custom DPI scaling logic in `DPI_Per_Monitor.cs`, `frmMain.cs`, and `DockPaneStripNG.cs` to resolve scaling issues on 4K monitors, especially for manually sized or positioned controls.
